opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: A sudden, violent pain ripped right between my shoulder blades and is now tearing its way down into my stomach and legs.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on out of nowhere about an hour ago, worst pain of my life, and now my left leg has gone numb and cold and I can't stand up.

symptom_narrative.onset: about an hour ago, completely out of the blue — it hit maximum straight away
symptom_narrative.duration: an hour, and over the last half hour it has been spreading down
symptom_narrative.character: a tearing, ripping pain, like something is being torn open
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst pain I have ever felt, I'm writhing on the floor
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: started right between my shoulder blades, now ripping down into my stomach, left side and down my legs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, and moving downwards
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any movement seems to make it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feeling sick in the stomach and profoundly unwell; my left leg has gone weak, numb and cold and feels dead
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never had anything like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm on the floor and can't get up — my left leg won't work

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: very high blood pressure that has been hard to control
past_medical_history.how_managed: supposed to be on blood pressure tablets

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; left the front door open so someone can get in to help

objective_data_offered.value: around 210/115 on my home blood pressure machine
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an uncalibrated home cuff taken while in severe distress on the floor — may be inaccurate

objective_data_offered.value: man lying supine on the living-room floor, sweating heavily and writhing, unable to stand
left leg looks pale compared with the right and the patient says it feels cold, numb and dead; he cannot move it properly
